data.content:
Plan for K-16:

1. Add `//go:build integration` test at internal/store/store_integration_test.go.
   Test plan:
   - t.TempDir() for the secrets root.
   - Resolve real `age` and `age-keygen` from PATH; t.Skip if missing so
     `go test ./...` in dev still passes.
   - Generate an identity via `age-keygen -o id.txt`, parse the public key
     from the `# public key:` header line.
   - store.New(root, identity); s.Init("svc","prod", []string{pubkey}).
   - SetSecret K1=v1; CreateSecret K2=v2; ListSecrets returns sorted [K1 K2].
   - Render returns "K1=v1\nK2=v2\n".
   - Generate a 2nd identity, AddRecipient its pubkey, Render still returns
     same plaintext, RemoveRecipient drops it back to one recipient.
   - Final RemoveRecipient on the last recipient must error (invariant).

2. Add an `integration` make target: `go test -tags integration -timeout 60s ./...`.

3. Add `integration` job to .github/workflows/ci.yml:
   - runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   - apt-get update && apt-get install -y age
   - actions/setup-go@v5 with go-version-file
   - go test -tags integration -timeout 60s ./...

Risks:
- age not available on stock ubuntu-latest images: apt-get works on jammy
  (22.04) and noble (24.04). ubuntu-latest currently maps to 24.04 which
  ships `age` in universe.
- Identity parsing: age-keygen writes `# public key: age1...` as the
  second line. Parse defensively (regex `age1[a-z0-9]+`).

Out of scope:
- macOS integration job (knot says Ubuntu sufficient).
- A new package under internal/age/*_integration_test.go (knot suggests
  store/ instead because the lifecycle goes through Store).

Effort: M.  Touches one new test file, one Makefile target, one CI job.
